qa_pairs.question: What are the default Globus sharing permissions for file systems on Alliance general-purpose clusters and the Trillium cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: On the Trillium cluster, Globus sharing is not enabled for any file systems. For general-purpose clusters, sharing is enabled for the `/home` directory (except on Rorqual), and it is not enabled for `/scratch` (except on Narval). Sharing for `/project` directories on general-purpose clusters is disabled by default but can be enabled on demand.

qa_pairs.question: How can a Principal Investigator (PI) request to enable Globus sharing for a `/project` directory on an Alliance general-purpose cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: To enable Globus sharing on a `/project` directory, the PI needs to contact `globus@tech.alliancecan.ca`. The request must include confirmation that sharing should be enabled, the name of the cluster, the specific path to enable (e.g., `/project/my-project-id/Sharing`), and whether the sharing will be read-only or read and write.

qa_pairs.question: When preparing data for a Globus shared collection, what is a crucial step to ensure the data is accessible and to avoid permission errors?
qa_pairs.answer: The data intended for sharing must be moved or copied directly to the designated path for the shared collection. Creating a symbolic link to the data will not allow access and will result in a permission error, stating that you do not have permission to create a shared endpoint on the selected path.
